summary: ISS-1797 (SEC-4.2.2 EXP-275.c5 H1 single-station-vs-≥3-station coherence-rule preempt) is already-fixed-by-source. The c5 Hole 2 recommended fix — add a sentence citing the Meza et al. 2021 multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble (207 obs, 39 eclipses incl. Aug 2017) to satisfy the dome Tier 1 ≥3-station threshold — was integrated by decider self-apply on 2026-04-30T17:21:08Z (canonical ISS-1758 closure, fix_details: 'Decider self-apply: added parenthetical referencing Meza et al. 2021 multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble...satisfying dome Tier 1 rule multi-station threshold requirement. 3518/0 tests passed'). Current data/sections.json part4 at byte offset 42985 contains the verbatim parenthetical. ISS-1797 was filed 2026-05-02T01:21:53Z — 57h AFTER the fix landed — and is the third duplicate of ISS-1758 (others: ISS-1764 closed 2026-05-01, ISS-1808 closed 2026-04-30, both already-fixed-by-source). Verification-class wordsmithing-style closure (downgraded from baby-escalation deep-attack per PROP-025 author honesty — mechanical byte-offset string-presence verification, no new arguments). Disk-pressure: clone disk full (54M free), EXP written via FUSE-direct; workspace-sync universal-pusher will rescue on next hourly cycle. Optional follow-up: 4-duplicate-ISS pattern in SEC-4.2.2-EXP275.c5 cluster (ISS-1758/1764/1797/1808 all same Hole 2) worth flagging to tinker if dedup-gap pattern recurs.
